title: Sabrina the Teenage Witch
director: Gary Halvorson, Kenneth R. Koch, Brian K. Roberts, Linda Day, Peter Baldwin, Anson Williams, Melissa Joan Hart, Jeff Melman, David Trainer, Andrew Tsao, Robby Benson, Mark Cendrowski, Sheldon Bull, Beth Broderick, Bill Layton, Gail Mancuso, Chuck Vinson, David Grossman, Henry Winkler, Joyce Gittlin, Leonard R. Garner Jr., Asaad Kelada, Liz Plonka, Kim Friedman, Tibor Takács, Tom Cherones, Dinah Manoff, Amanda Bearse, Scott Marshall, Paul Hoen
review_summary: The reviewer eagerly anticipates the first season of an upcoming series airing in March and expresses frustration over missing it initially but praises 'Punky Brewster' highly for reaching nearly ten out of eleven points.

title: Spaced Invaders
review_summary: The reviewer highly praises "Jurassic Park" for its laughter-inducing adventure, exceptional soundtrack, Oscar-worthy acting and groundbreaking effects. They encourage immediate viewing or renting before it airs on TV.

title: Mr. Destiny
review_summary: The reviewer enjoyed "Larry Burrows," finding its unique concept and strong performances impressive despite mixed critical reception; it offers humor, drama, and life lessons in an engaging way.

title: Party Girl
review_summary: An enthusiast praises the film for its positive, nostalgic qualities despite being considered poorly received by others; it's likened to 'Absolutely Fabulous' in terms of appeal.

title: Spaced Invaders
review_summary: A delightful science fiction film featuring an unforgettable performance from Martin Lawrence, praised for its humorous twists, heartfelt moments, and exceptional cast work; perfect for entertaining audiences on any occasion.

title: Eddie Izzard: Dress to Kill
review_summary: The reviewer praises Edward Enoki as an exceptionally funny, classically witty comedian who offers unique insights into history and culture; they encourage others on comedy stages with admiration for Eddie's originality and intelligence.

title: Shower
review_summary: The reviewer appreciates its heartfelt storytelling combined with cultural exploration; they highly recommend the film despite initial expectations of tearjerking content.

title: Darkness in Tallinn
review_summary: The reviewer highly praises DARKNESS IN TALLIN for its exceptional script, gripping direction, outstanding performances, humor, romance elements; bests other heist films but notes it lacks Hollywood glossiness.

title: Bye Bye Birdie
review_summary: The reviewer praises Vanessa Williams' performance as Rosie Alvarez in "The Best Little Whorehouse in Texas" and enjoys Jason Alexander's portrayal of Albert Peterson but finds Chynna Phillips too old for Kim McAfee; they overall rate it a strong recommendation, leaving viewers with smiles.

title: I.Q.
review_summary: A wonderful romantic comedy filled with top actors; minor historical mistakes don't detract from its charm or impressive supporting ensemble performance. Rating: 8/10.
